title: Static site: jurisdiction selector/label, 'not legal advice' + route-to-help blocks, print stylesheet
context: Know-Your-Rights publishes its attorney-reviewed content as a static, accessible, no-JS-required website. Every page must carry a visible jurisdiction label and selector (so readers never mistake one state's law for another's), the persistent 'not legal advice' banner with a route-to-help block, a lastVerified stamp, and a staleness indicator for any content past its validUntil window. The print stylesheet must produce the offline wallet card. Depends on tasks 003 and 009.
objective: Implement the publishing surface (apps/site) as a static, accessible, no-JS-required site that renders the pilot jurisdiction's tenant-rights content with: an explicit jurisdiction label on every page (and a jurisdiction selector for multi-jurisdiction expansion), the persistent 'not legal advice' + route-to-help blocks, a lastVerified stamp, staleness flags or withheld display for past-validUntil content, and a print stylesheet that produces the offline wallet card.
acceptanceCriteria: Every page displays an explicit jurisdiction label and the persistent 'This is general legal information, not legal advice. Laws change and vary by location. For your situation, consult a lawyer or your local legal aid.' banner with a route-to-help block (legal aid, housing court self-help, or relevant agency links)
Every page stamps lastVerified; content past validUntil is displayed with a staleness flag ('this may be out of date — verify before relying') or withheld, per the fail-safe from task 007
All pages render correctly with JavaScript disabled (no-JS fallback required for core content)
Print stylesheet produces a legible, one-page offline wallet card matching the card content from task 010
Jurisdiction selector/label is wired to the content schema's jurisdiction field; adding a second jurisdiction in the future requires only adding content files, not changing site infrastructure
pnpm build && pnpm test pass; site builds to static HTML + CSS with no server-side runtime required
Reviewed by maintainer and editorial reviewer; layout sign-off recorded
output: A merged PR implementing the static publishing surface (apps/site) with jurisdiction label, 'not legal advice' banner, route-to-help block, lastVerified stamp, staleness fail-safe display, and a print stylesheet for the offline wallet card.
